sections.section_title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls

items.requirement_raw: Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained?
items.help_text: Network security controls (NSCs) are configured and maintained.

items.requirement_raw: All services, protocols and ports allowed are identified, approved, and have a defined business need.

items.help_text: All services, protocols and ports allowed are identified, approved, and have a defined business need.

items.requirement_raw: Security features are defined and implemented for all services, protocols, and ports that are in use and considered to be insecure, such that the risk is mitigated.

items.help_text: Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted.

items.requirement_raw: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted as follows: To only traffic that is necessary. All other traffic is specifically denied.

items.help_text: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted as follows: To only traffic that is necessary. All other traffic is specifically denied.

items.requirement_raw: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted as follows: To only traffic that is necessary. All other traffic is specifically denied.

items.help_text: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted as follows: To only traffic that is necessary. All other traffic is specifically denied.

Only wireless traffic with an authorized business purpose is allowed into the CDE.

sections.section_title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

items.requirement_raw: System components are configured and managed securely.

items.help_text: System components are configured and managed securely.

sections.section_title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission Over Open, Public Networks

items.requirement_raw: PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission.

items.requirement_raw: Strong cryptography and security protocols are implemented as follows to safeguard PAN during transmission over open, public networks: Only trusted keys and certificates are accepted. • Examine documented policies and procedures.

sections.section_title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software

items.requirement_raw: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed. Note: For SAQ B-IP, this requirement applies to the merchant’s firewall/router devices that connect PTS POI devices to the payment processor. Identification and management of security vulnerabilities for PTS POI devices are often handled by the merchant’s terminal provider or processor. The merchant should contact the entity managing its terminals to understand how this requirement is met and the responsibilities of the merchant and of the entity managing the terminals.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed. Note: For SAQ B-IP, this requirement applies to the merchant’s firewall/router devices that connect PTS POI devices to the payment processor. Identification and management of security vulnerabilities for PTS POI devices are often handled by the merchant’s terminal provider or processor. The merchant should contact the entity managing its terminals to understand how this requirement is met and the responsibilities of the merchant and of the entity managing the terminals?
items.help_text: Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed. Note: For SAQ B-IP, this requirement applies to the merchant’s firewall/router devices that connect PTS POI devices to the payment processor. Identification and management of security vulnerabilities...

items.requirement_raw: Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed as follows: New security vulnerabilities are identified using industry-recognized sources for security vulnerability information, including alerts from international and national computer emergency response teams (CERTs). Vulnerabilities are assigned a risk ranking based on industry best practices and consideration of potential impact. Risk rankings identify, at a minimum, all vulnerabilities considered to be a high-risk or critical to the environment. Bullet intentionally left blank for this SAQ.

sections.section_title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly

items.requirement_raw: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that external and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed?
items.help_text: External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.

items.requirement_raw: External vulnerability scans are performed as follows: At least once every three months. By a PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). Vulnerabilities are resolved and ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan are met. Rescans are performed as needed to confirm that vulnerabilities are resolved per the ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan.
items.question: Do you perform required vulnerability scans and address any findings?
items.help_text: External vulnerability scans are performed as follows: At least once every three months. By a PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). Vulnerabilities are resolved and ASV Program Guide requirements for a passing scan are met. Rescans are performed as...

items.question: Do you perform penetration testing when required and after significant changes?
items.help_text: External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed, and exploitable vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected.

items.requirement_raw: If segmentation is used to isolate the CDE from other networks, penetration tests are performed on segmentation controls as follows: At least once every 12 months and after any changes to segmentation controls/methods. Confirming that the segmentation controls/methods are operational and effective, and isolate the CDE from all out-of-scope systems. Confirming effectiveness of any use of isolation to separate systems with differing security levels (see Requirement 2.2.3). Performed by a qualified internal resource or qualified external third party. Organizational independence of the tester exists (not required to be a QSA or ASV).

Performed by a qualified internal resource or qualified external third party. Organizational independence of the tester exists (not required to be a QSA or ASV)?

sections.section_title: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs

items.requirement_raw: A comprehensive information security policy that governs and provides direction for protection of the entity’s information assets is known and current.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that a comprehensive information security policy that governs and provides direction for protection of the entity’s information assets is known and current?
items.help_text: A comprehensive information security policy that governs and provides direction for protection of the entity’s information assets is known and current.

items.requirement_raw: An overall information security policy is: Established. Published. Maintained. Disseminated to all relevant personnel, as well as to relevant vendors and business partners.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that an overall information security policy is: Established. Published. Maintained. Disseminated to all relevant personnel, as well as to relevant vendors and business partners?
items.help_text: An overall information security policy is: Established. Published. Maintained. Disseminated to all relevant personnel, as well as to relevant vendors and business partners.

items.requirement_raw: The information security policy is:
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that the information security policy is:?
items.help_text: The information security policy is: SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Reviewed at least once every 12 months.
Updated as needed to reflect changes to business
objectives or risks to the environment.
Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirements 12.1.1 and 12.1.2 means that the merchant has a security policy that is reasonable for the size and
complexity of the merchant’s operations, and that the policy is reviewed at least once every 12 months and updated if needed.
For example, such a policy could be a simple document that covers how to protect the store and payment devices in accordance with the solution provider’s
guidance/instruction manual, and who to call in an emergency.

items.requirement_raw: The security policy clearly defines information security roles and responsibilities for all personnel, and all personnel are aware of and acknowledge their information security responsibilities.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that the security policy clearly defines information security roles and responsibilities for all personnel, and all personnel are aware of and acknowledge their information security responsibilities?
items.help_text: The security policy clearly defines information security roles and responsibilities for all personnel, and all personnel are aware of and acknowledge their information security responsibilities. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirement 12.1.3 means that the merchant’s security policy defines basic security responsibilities for all personnel,
consistent with the size and complexity of the merchant’s operations. For example, security responsibilities could be defined according to basic responsibilities by
employee levels, such as the responsibilities expected of a manager/owner and those expected of clerks.

items.requirement_raw: Security awareness education is an ongoing activity.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that security awareness education is an ongoing activity?
items.help_text: Security awareness education is an ongoing activity.

items.requirement_raw: A formal security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the entity’s information security policy and procedures, and their role in protecting the cardholder data.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that a formal security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the entity’s information security policy and procedures, and their role in protecting the cardholder data?
items.help_text: A formal security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the entity’s information security policy and procedures, and their role in protecting the cardholder data. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirement 12.6.1 means that the merchant has a security awareness program in place, consistent with the size and
complexity of the merchant’s business operations. For example, a simple awareness program could be a flyer posted in the back office, or a periodic e-mail sent to all
employees. Examples of awareness program messaging include descriptions of security tips all employees should follow, such as how to lock doors and storage
containers, how to determine whether a payment terminal has been tampered with, and processes to confirm the identity and verify there is a legitimate business
reason for any service workers when they arrive to service payment terminals.

items.requirement_raw: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Risk to information assets associated with third-party service provider (TPSP) relationships is managed.

items.requirement_raw: A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of account data is maintained, including a description for each of the services provided.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of account data is maintained, including a description for each of the services provided.

items.requirement_raw: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained as follows: Written agreements are maintained with all TPSPs with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of the CDE. Written agreements include acknowledgments from TPSPs that TPSPs are responsible for the security of account data the TPSPs possess or otherwise store, process, or transmit on behalf of the entity, or to the extent that the TPSP could impact the security of the entity’s cardholder data and/or sensitive authentication data.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Written agreements with TPSPs are maintained as follows: Written agreements are maintained with all TPSPs with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of the CDE. Written agreements include acknowledgments from TPSPs that TPSPs are...

items.requirement_raw: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence prior to engagement.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: An established process is implemented for engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence prior to engagement.

items.requirement_raw: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs’ PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs’ PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12 months.

items.requirement_raw: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the entity.
items.question: Do you manage third-party service providers with clear oversight, agreements, and responsibility tracking?
items.help_text: Information is maintained about which PCI DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity, and any that are shared between the TPSP and the entity. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for requirements at 12.8.1 through 12.8.5 means that the merchant has a list of, and agreements with, service providers it
shares account data with or that could impact the security of the merchant’s cardholder data environment. For example, such agreements would be applicable if a
merchant uses a document-retention company to store paper documents that include account data or if a merchant’s vendor accesses merchant systems remotely to

items.requirement_raw: Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.
items.question: Do you have controls in place to ensure that suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately?
items.help_text: Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.

items.requirement_raw: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident.
items.question: Do you have a documented incident response plan that is current and ready to use?
items.help_text: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident. SAQ Completion Guidance:
expected_testing_raw: Selection of any of the In Place responses for Requirement 12.10.1 means that the merchant has documented an incident response and escalation plan to be used for
emergencies, consistent with the size and complexity of the merchant’s operations. For example, such a plan could be a simple document posted in the back office
that lists who to call in the event of various situations with an annual review to confirm it is still accurate, but could extend all the way to a full incident response plan
including backup “hotsite” facilities and thorough annual testing. This plan should be readily available to all personnel as a resource in an emergency.

sections.section_title: Additional PCI DSS Requirements for Entities using SSL/Early TLS for Card-Present POS POI Terminal

items.requirement_raw: Where POS POI terminals at the merchant or payment acceptance location use SSL and/or early TLS, the entity confirms the devices are not susceptible to any known exploits for those protocols. Examine documentation (for example, vendor documentation, system/network configuration details) that verifies the devices are not susceptible to any known exploits for SSL/early TLS.
